pendingTriage.author: Andy_NO
pendingTriage.contentPreview: Originally posted by CJS 					 						 						 							 							 							 							 								 								 								 								 								 									 										 									 								 							 						 					 				 				There was a problem with installation of version 2.3.1. Now version 2.3.2 is out and in my case that version
pendingTriage.draft:
Hi Andy_NO,

Sorry to hear you're having trouble getting SpotOn to show up properly!

A couple of things to try:

[B]1. Check your LMS version[/B]
SpotOn requires LMS 9.0 or later. If you're on an older version, the plugin may silently fail to register and could cause instability. You can check under [B]Settings → Information[/B].

[B]2. Force a clean reinstall[/B]
Since you're stuck in a reboot loop and can't uninstall through the UI, try removing the plugin files manually:

[CODE]
# Find and delete the SpotOn plugin folder, typically under:
# <LMS data dir>/Cache/InstalledPlugins/Plugins/SpotOn/
# or
# <LMS install dir>/Plugins/SpotOn/
[/CODE]

Then restart LMS once manually and try reinstalling from the plugin repository.

[B]3. Check the LMS server log[/B]
The reboot loop is the key symptom here — something is causing LMS to crash or restart repeatedly. Please check your LMS server log ([B]Settings → Information → Server Log[/B]) for any error messages around the time of the restarts. Look for lines mentioning SpotOn or any Perl errors.

[B]4. Grab a diagnostic bundle[/B]
If SpotOn does manage to load even partially, go to [B]Settings → Advanced → SpotOn → Diagnostics[/B] and copy the bundle here — it'll give a clearer picture of what's happening.

Given the constant reboot behaviour, it would be very helpful to see the server log. Could you paste any relevant error lines here?

Also, please consider filing a bug report on GitHub with your log output so this can be tracked properly:
[URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL]

pendingTriage.author: CJS
pendingTriage.contentPreview: Originally posted by Andy_NO 					 						 						 							 							 							 							 								 								 								 								 								 									 										 									 								 							 						 					 				 				But there is reboot after reboot. And I do not see Spotin in the list of plugins. I couldnt even de-inst
pendingTriage.draft:
Great advice from Andy_NO — a clean manual uninstall is usually the best fix when a plugin gets stuck in a broken state and won't appear in the list.

Just to add a bit more detail on finding the right paths:

[B]Common LMS Cache locations by OS:[/B]

[CODE]
Windows:   C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Local\Squeezebox\Cache
Linux:     /var/lib/squeezeboxserver/cache  (or check LMS → Settings → Information)
macOS:     ~/Library/Application Support/Squeezebox/Cache
Docker:    Depends on your volume mapping — check your container config
[/CODE]

Once you've done the manual cleanup and LMS starts cleanly, you should see SpotOn available again in [B]Settings → Manage Plugins[/B] under the "Available Plugins" section.

[B]A couple of things to check after reinstalling:[/B]
[LIST]
[*]Make sure you're on [B]2.3.5[/B] (the latest) — earlier 2.3.x versions had some installation issues
[*]After install, a full LMS restart (not just a plugin reload) is recommended
[*]Confirm SpotOn appears under [B]Settings → My Apps[/B] before trying to configure it
[/LIST]

If it still won't install cleanly after following Andy's steps, it would be really helpful to know:
[LIST]
[*]Which OS / platform are you running LMS on?
[*]Any relevant errors in the LMS server log ([B]Settings → Advanced → Logging[/B])?
[/LIST]

If you're still stuck, please open a bug report at [URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL] and include your OS, LMS version, and any log output — that'll help us track down what's going wrong.

pendingTriage.author: plasticator2
pendingTriage.contentPreview: sti Thanks for the prompt reply! The story so far. Main listening room is the lounge, Pi 4B player/server, output through Hifiberry Digi2 pro SPDIF to external DAC. Squeezelite ALSA device name is hw:CARD=sndrpihifiberry,DEV=0. No replay gain. I already tried all permutations, but repeated today wit
pendingTriage.draft:
plasticator2 That's a really interesting finding — thank you for the systematic testing! The fact that syncing the Pi to the Windows/Squeezelite-X player produces perfect audio is a valuable clue.

This strongly suggests the issue is specific to the [B]HifiBerry Digi2 Pro / SPDIF output path[/B] on the Pi, rather than SpotOn's audio pipeline itself. A few thoughts:

[B]Most likely culprit: Sample rate or bit depth mismatch[/B]
SPDIF is strict about clock rates. If SpotOn is sending a sample rate that the HifiBerry or your DAC isn't expecting (or can't negotiate cleanly), you'll get exactly the kind of loud/distorted output you're describing. This can happen even if the same setup works fine with local files.

Can you check:
[CODE]
cat /proc/asound/sndrpihifiberry/pcm0p/sub0/hw_params
[/CODE]
...while audio is actively playing via SpotOn? This will show the actual sample rate and format being sent to the device.

[B]Things worth trying:[/B]
[LIST]
[*]In SpotOn player settings, try forcing [B]PCM format at 44100Hz[/B] if there's a rate option — this gives the SPDIF chain least room to misbehave
[*]Check if your DAC has a sample rate indicator — does it show a sane value during playback?
[*]Try setting Squeezelite's [B]-r 44100[/B] flag to lock the output rate
[/LIST]

[B]The log file[/B] — could you paste the relevant section here or attach it? I want to look for any resampling or format negotiation errors around the point playback starts.

If this turns out to be a reproducible bug, please do open a report at [URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL] with the hw_params output and log — it'll help track it properly.

pendingTriage.author: plasticator2
pendingTriage.contentPreview: Paul WebsterThanks Paul. Post 143 edited and log file now attached
pendingTriage.draft:
Thanks for attaching the log file, plasticator2!

I can see Paul is already looking into this with you — I'll let that thread continue and jump in if needed.

Just to make sure I have the full picture from post #143: you're seeing loud/distorted audio across [B]all[/B] format options (OGG, FLAC, PCM, auto) on your Pi 4B + Hifiberry Digi2 Pro setup, but you mentioned "here it gets interesting..." — your post appears to have been cut off there. Could you complete that thought? The detail you were about to share might be important for diagnosing whether this is a format/transcoding issue or something specific to the SPDIF output path.

A few things that would help narrow it down while we wait:

[B]From the diagnostics bundle[/B] (SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics), please check:
[LIST]
[*]What sample rate and bit depth SpotOn is negotiating with Squeezelite
[*]Any resampling warnings in the SpotOn log
[/LIST]

[B]Quick sanity check:[/B] Does playback sound correct when using a [B]different source[/B] (e.g., local files or another plugin) through the same Hifiberry → DAC chain? This would confirm whether the distortion is SpotOn-specific or a system-level audio configuration issue.

[CODE]hw:CARD=sndrpihifiberry,DEV=0[/CODE]

That ALSA device string looks correct for the Digi2 Pro, so the card name itself shouldn't be the culprit — but it's worth double-checking the SPDIF output isn't being fed an unexpected sample rate that your DAC is mishandling.

If this turns out to be a reproducible bug, please do open a report at [URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL] with the log attached — it'll help track it properly.
